items.title: Notice: CCUS East Coast Cluster: Teesside selection process
items.summary: Apply to take part in the ECC Teesside selection process for projects seeking connection to the Teesside network by 2032.

items.title: NHS patients and British businesses to benefit from historic changes to medicines access following pharmaceutical partnership with USA
items.summary: New partnership accelerates NHS patients' access to new medicines and will see UK benefit from greater life sciences industry investment

items.title: Decision: North Killingholme Power Project: non-material change, Planning Act 2008
items.summary: Decision by the Secretary of State on an application for a non-material change relating to a new thermal generating station that will operate as a Combined Cycle Gas Turbine (CCGT) plant.

items.title: Warm Home Discount (WHD): cost recovery
items.summary: We are seeking views on whether WHD costs should move from standing charges to the per-unit rate.

items.title: Official Statistics: Preference utilisation of UK trade in goods, 2024
items.summary: This statistics release will provide data and analysis of the extent to which UK goods imports and exports utilised tariff preferences under preferential trade agreements (PTAs) in 2024, including the EU-UK Trade and Cooperation Agreement (TCA), and the Developing Countries Trading Scheme (DCTS).

items.title: Oil and gas: offshore environmental legislation
items.summary: Environmental regulations and guidance on offshore oil and gas exploration and production offshore gas unloading and storage and offshore carbon dioxide storage activities

items.title: Decision: UK's steel trade measure from 1 July 2026
items.summary: Details about a new steel trade measure from 1 July 2026, which will limit tariff-free steel import quota volumes.

items.title: Official Statistics: Warm Homes: Social Housing Fund statistics - April 2026
items.summary: Data to monitor the installation of energy efficiency measures in domestic properties via the Warm Homes: Social Housing Fund scheme in England.

items.title: Official Statistics: Warm Homes: Local Grant statistics - April 2026
items.summary: Data to monitor the installation of energy efficiency measures in domestic properties via the Warm Homes: Local Grant scheme in England.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Renewables obligation: certificates and generation: December 2025
items.summary: Monthly data on the number of certificates issued for generation under the Renewables Obligation.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Energy trends: October to December 2026
items.summary: Energy production, consumption and trade of total energy, coal, oil, gas, electricity and renewables during the fourth calendar quarter of 2026.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: UK greenhouse gas emissions: 2026 provisional figures
items.summary: Provisional estimates of UK territorial greenhouse gas emissions based on provisional inland energy consumption statistics, which are published in the quarterly Energy Trends statistical publication.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Energy trends and prices: January 2027
items.summary: Monthly production, trade and consumption of coal, electricity, gas, oil and total energy; retail price data, petrol & diesel price data and EU comparative price data.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Energy prices: October to December 2026
items.summary: Energy prices to domestic and industrial consumers for all the major fuels, with international comparisons, for the fourth calendar quarter of 2026.

items.title: Transparency data: DESNZ: prompt payment data, October to December 2025
items.summary: How long the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) takes to pay invoices.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Provisional UK greenhouse gas emissions statistics 2025
items.summary: Provisional estimates of UK territorial greenhouse gas emissions for 2025, including quarterly emissions estimates.

items.title: Guidance: List of special feature articles by subject published in Energy Trends
items.summary: List of special feature articles by subject published in Energy Trends since 2017.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Clean Power 2030 metrics
items.summary: Data on the progress against the Clean Power 2030 metrics as published in Energy Trends.

items.title: Research: Energy Trends: March 2026, special feature article - Review of solid biomass classifications
items.summary: Special feature article from the March 2026 edition of Energy Trends statistical publication.

items.title: Research: Energy Trends: March 2026, special feature article
items.summary: Special feature article on energy issues in the United Kingdom from the March 2026 edition of the quarterly statistical bulletin Energy Trends.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Energy Trends and Prices statistical release: 2 April 2026
items.summary: The latest provisional monthly energy production, trade, electricity generation, consumption and prices statistics produced by the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Energy Trends: March 2026
items.summary: ‘Energy Trends’ is a quarterly bulletin containing statistics on all major aspects of energy in the UK.

items.title: Guidance: Quarterly total energy data revisions
items.summary: A zip file containing revisions to the quarterly total energy statistical data (excel format).

items.title: Consultation on the implementation of the new subscription contracts regime
items.summary: We are seeking views on our legislative proposals for subscription contracts between traders and consumers. This consultation will inform secondary legislation.

items.title: Correspondence: Keep Britain Working Review updates
items.summary: Updates on the independent review of the role of employers in tackling health based economic inactivity and promoting healthy and inclusive workplaces.

items.title: Consumers to save around £400 million every year from government crackdown on costly subscription traps
items.summary: Hard working Brits will keep more of their cash under a new government crackdown on unwanted and misleading subscriptions

items.title: Notice: Notice to exporters 2026/08: general trade licence Russia sanctions – sectoral software and technology
items.summary: The Export Control Joint Unit (ECJU) has updated the general trade licence Russia sanctions for sectoral software and technology.

items.title: Guidance: General trade licence Russia sanctions: sectoral software and technology
items.summary: This is a general trade licence in relation to prohibitions under Chapter 4N of the Russia Sanctions regulations on business enterprise software and technology.

items.title: Form: Contracts for Difference, renewables obligation and small scale feed-in tariffs: apply for an exemption or compensation
items.summary: How to apply for an exemption or compensation for a proportion of the indirect costs of funding the Contracts for Difference, renewables obligation and small scale feed-in tariffs.

items.title: Guidance: Using harmonised standards to place CE marked products on the market in Great Britain: UKCA and CE regimes
items.summary: Find out more about continued recognition of the Conformité Européene (CE) marking, and whether as a manufacturer you need to take additional steps to place your product on the market in Great Britain.

items.title: Guidance: Apply for a Canton-specific work permit for Switzerland
items.summary: Canton specific guidance on applying for a work permit for Switzerland.

items.title: Policy paper: Product safety and metrology regulatory changes: UKCA and CE regimes
items.summary: Read more about changes to the overarching regulatory regime for product safety and metrology, following the UK’s exit from the European Union.

items.summary: Where to find information on what you need to do to comply with regulations which govern manufactured products placed on the market in the European Union.

items.title: Fast-Track UKCA: UKCA and CE regimes

items.summary: What you need to do to comply with regulations which govern manufactured products placed on the Northern Ireland market.

items.summary: Guidance on product marking for specific product sectors, including the continued recognition of certain EU requirements, such as CE marking.

items.title: Placing UKCA or CE marked products on the market in Great Britain
items.summary: What you need to do to comply with regulations which govern manufactured products placed on the market in Great Britain.

items.title: Product regulation: fire safety of domestic upholstered furniture
items.summary: We are seeking final views on the comprehensive reform of the Furniture and Furnishings (Fire) (Safety) Regulations 1988.

items.title: Product regulation: the UK’s new product safety framework
items.summary: We are seeking views from businesses, consumer groups and all other interested stakeholders on a new, modernised and enhanced core product safety framework.

items.title: Product regulation: market surveillance and enforcement framework
items.summary: We are seeking views on proposed reforms to UK product regulation market surveillance and enforcement.
